primary.notes: Single-draft calibration fixture — LI-6 unscorable as a true cohort dimension (N=1), defaulted to neutral 5. Main quality lever holding aggregate down is LI-1's automatic jargon cap ('LHR-style' with no plain-English gloss) — fixing that one phrase would lift LI-1 to ~8 and aggregate to ~7.0. Structural pass: 1647 chars inside THOUGHT_LEADER 1500-2500 bracket, 4 hashtags inside [3,5]. Grounding pass: gofreddy named entity throughout, attribution to PR #45/0543d7b in META, interpretive claims framed as JR's view. No HARD-FLOOR triggers (no unnamed-entity lived-work claims, no contradictions of source_text).

per_criterion.rationale: Voice is genuinely thoughtful-authority register: first-person, story-led colleague-asked opener, B2B-buyer accessible. Tone reads 'I keep returning to' (lived-work) not 'most marketers don't realize' (contrarian). However, 'triaged 9-section LHR-style report' uses LHR jargon with no plain-English follow-up, and '149 always-on lenses plus 25 vertical bundles' is internal product jargon a CMO reader cannot parse. Per the rubric's AUTOMATIC ≤6 floor for jargon without inline plain-English follow-up, the dimension caps at 6 even though everything else here would score higher.

Lived-work claims name 'gofreddy' as the product (assumed present in voice.md as it's JR's own platform — no HARD-FLOOR violation). Specific numerics ('around fifty cents', '33 actionable findings', '20 verified', '47 hours of agent time', '36-finding deliverable across 11 marketing areas') are framed as JR's own pipeline output, with attribution to 'gofreddy harness PR #45 squash 0543d7b' in META. Interpretive claims ('treat compute as inventory', 'AI-native pricing does not look like agency-hours pricing') are framed as JR's view, not declared facts. The 'colleague' is unnamed but used as story framing, not as a factual claim. No vague-specific cap-at-7 trigger because the named entity (gofreddy) is consistently present.

Opening line is ~80 chars, well within the ~210 desktop show-more cutoff. Story-led with question framing ('A few weeks ago a colleague asked: how does an AI-native agency actually price work?'). However, the hook lacks the specific concrete grounding the rubric's Score-5 anchor calls for ('Last quarter we rebuilt... shipped 5 features in two weeks'). The colleague-asked-question pattern is recognizable but generic; the lived-work anchor (numbers, gofreddy specifics) only arrives in paragraph 2. 'Three answers I keep returning to' earns line two adequately but not strongly. Sits between Score 3 (formulaic concrete-number opener) and Score 5 (story-led with concrete grounding) — score 6.

No banned LinkedIn-AI tells: no 'Game-changer', no 'Here's what I learned' alone-line close, no engagement-bait emoji prompts ('Thoughts? 👇'), no whitespace-inflation paragraph breaks, no fake 'Hot take:' framing. Paragraph rhythm is prose, not whitespace-padded listicle. One mild thought-leadership cadence pattern slips in: 'If you take one thing away:' as the closing transition marker — formulaic but not egregious. 'Three answers I keep returning to' is also a slight cadence marker but the prose-numbered structure (First/Second/Third) is appropriate for THOUGHT_LEADER bracket rather than AI-tell. Lands at 7 — mostly clean voice with one minor formulaic close.

Char count 1647 sits inside THOUGHT_LEADER bracket (1500-2500). Structure executes the bracket spec: story opening (colleague-asked) → frame ('Three answers I keep returning to') → 3 numbered points (First/Second/Third with concrete substance under each) → implication close ('If you take one thing away: AI-native pricing does not look like agency-hours pricing'). Each point carries specific lived-work grounding (fifty-cent compute, 33-finding harness run, 36-finding deliverable). Hashtag count = 4 (B2BMarketing, AIagents, MarketingOps, AgencyPricing) — in the [3,5] ideal range, no penalty cap. Hashtags map to brand-pillar topics, not generic '#marketing'. Strong bracket-aware execution.

Only one draft in this session's drafts/ directory (calibration-thought_leader). Cohort variance cannot be meaningfully assessed with N=1. The single draft uses a lesson-led / case-study hybrid archetype (colleague-question framing + three numbered lessons + named-system grounding). Voice pillar 'harness-engineering' is consistent with the metadata. Per the rubric's geometric-mean construction across drafts, a single-draft cohort defaults to a neutral mid-score; cannot demonstrate same-tone-streak failure (Score 1) nor cross-archetype variance (Score 5). Score 5 reflects 'unscorable as cohort, single draft is internally coherent' rather than evidence of breadth or repetition.

secondary.notes: Strongest dimensions are LinkedIn tone discipline and absence of AI tells. Main weaknesses are unverifiable factual grounding from the provided artifacts and lack of cohort evidence for LI-6.

per_criterion.rationale: Professional and mostly LinkedIn-appropriate: 'A few weeks ago a colleague asked' and 'Three answers I keep returning to' create a calm, first-person frame. It is not bait-y or contrarian. However, jargon like '149 always-on lenses,' 'deterministic check,' 'multi-cycle harness,' and 'LHR-style report' appears without enough plain-English follow-up, which caps the voice dimension.

The draft contains many specific factual claims: '149 always-on lenses plus 25 vertical bundles,' 'fifty cents,' '33 actionable findings,' '20 of them in 47 hours,' and '36-finding deliverable across 11 marketing areas.' Some lived-work claims name gofreddy, but the required voice.md/source material is not in the artifacts, so these cannot be cleanly verified from what is in front of me. The specificity is useful, but grounding is incomplete.

The opener is LinkedIn-suitable and earns the next line: 'A few weeks ago a colleague asked: how does an AI-native agency actually price work?' It has a lived-work setup and a clear business question. It is not hot-take bait. The limitation is that it is more topic-led than story-led; the colleague moment is thin and the first two lines quickly become a framework.

No obvious LinkedIn-AI tells like 'Game-changer,' 'Thoughts?,' emoji engagement bait, fake hot take framing, or whitespace padding. 'Three answers I keep returning to' has a slightly formulaic thought-leadership cadence, but the rest reads controlled and specific rather than AI-LinkedIn generic.

The THOUGHT_LEADER structure is coherent: setup, three framed points, implication close, and 4 targeted hashtags in metadata. The numbered-point structure is substantive and business-relevant. It loses some credit because the body does not visibly include the hashtags, and the structure is closer to a clean 3-point framework than a richer story-to-frame-to-implication piece.

Only one draft is provided in the session artifacts, so cohort variation cannot be demonstrated. The single draft uses a lesson-led/framework archetype and one visible voice pillar, 'harness-engineering.' Since no other drafts are in front of me, I cannot credit archetype or pillar spread.
